ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., contributor, validator, admin) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date the user registered */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date the user last logged in */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Account status (e.g., active, suspended, banned) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_profiles" (
  "profile_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user profile */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user the profile belongs to */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Biographical information about the user */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL to the user's website */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "about_me" TEXT /* Detailed biography of the user */,
  "social_media_links" TEXT /* JSON object containing links to the user's social media profiles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("profile_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_profiles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_entries" (
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who submitted the entry */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category the entry belongs to */,
  "entry_type" TEXT /* Type of the data entry (e.g., annotation, translation) */,
  "entry_data" TEXT /* The actual data content of the entry */,
  "submission_date" TEXT /* Date the entry was submitted */,
  "submission_time" TEXT /* Time the entry was submitted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the entry (e.g., pending, valid, invalid) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the entry was last updated */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data entry (e.g., API, web form) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entries_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entries_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES entry_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "entry_validations" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation event */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being validated */,
  "validator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who validated the entry */,
  "validation_date" TEXT /* Date the entry was validated */,
  "validation_time" TEXT /* Time the entry was validated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the entry is valid */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments provided by the validator */,
  "revalidation_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the entry has been revalidated */,
  "revalidation_comments" TEXT /* Comments from revalidations */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_entry_validations_validator_id FOREIGN KEY ("validator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_entry_validations_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "valid_entries" INTEGER /* Number of valid entries submitted by the user */,
  "invalid_entries" INTEGER /* Number of invalid entries submitted by the user */,
  "invalid_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of invalid entries submitted by the user */,
  "total_entries" INTEGER /* Total number of entries submitted by the user */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the user's entries */,
  "last_performance_update" TEXT /* Date the performance metrics were last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_performance_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "leaderboards" (
  "leaderboard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each leaderboard entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "rank" INTEGER /* Rank of the user */,
  "valid_entries" INTEGER /* Number of valid entries submitted by the user */,
  "invalid_entries" INTEGER /* Number of invalid entries submitted by the user */,
  "invalid_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of invalid entries submitted by the user */,
  "total_entries" INTEGER /* Total number of entries submitted by the user */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the user's entries */,
  "last_leaderboard_update" TEXT /* Date the leaderboard was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("leaderboard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_leaderboards_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of the notification (e.g., entry validation, performance update) */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date the notification was sent */,
  "notification_time" TEXT /* Time the notification was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the notification has been read */,
  "notification_source" TEXT /* Source of the notification (e.g., system, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality_checks" (
  "check_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being checked */,
  "check_type" TEXT /* Type of the quality check (e.g., spelling, grammar) */,
  "check_result" INTEGER /* Boolean flag indicating if the check passed */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date the check was performed */,
  "check_time" TEXT /* Time the check was performed */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments provided by the system or administrator */,
  "check_source" TEXT /* Source of the check (e.g., automated, manual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("check_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checks_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_entry_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history record */,
  "entry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being tracked */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., edit, delete) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the action was performed */,
  "history_time" TEXT /* Time the action was performed */,
  "previous_data" TEXT /* Previous data content before the action */,
  "new_data" TEXT /* New data content after the action */,
  "action_source" TEXT /* Source of the action (e.g., user, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entry_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_entry_history_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES data_entries ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "entry_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "category_order" INTEGER /* Order of the category in the list */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object containing permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date the role was assigned */,
  "assignment_source" TEXT /* Source of the role assignment (e.g., system, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_role_assignments_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_role_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
